pages.bodyText: All Posts price transparencyhospitalsCMScomplianceSumHealth Search Price Transparency: Bringing Clarity to the Cost of Health Why pricing transparency is a fundamental patient right, how the Hospital Price Transparency Rule and its penalties work, and how SumHealth Search makes hospital pricing data clear and comparable. Dr. Dhruvil Patel February 14, 2024 · 9 min read Money and Healthcare Within the increasingly complex web of healthcare in the United States, the issue of pricing transparency is more than a matter of fiscal prudence; it’s a fundamental right for patients and a cornerstone for fair and competitive services. The labyrinthine nature of hospital pricing systems has long been a source of frustration, confusion, and financial hardship for millions of Americans: from those receiving care to those employed by the system attempting to do right by their patients. In the United States, and in emerging healthcare services around the world the both health and the financial cost of achieving it intertwined. The economics of a healthcare system determine its ability to provide, achieve, and thereby directly impact its outcomes. This focus on cost has emerged a growing recognition of the scale of money that is required to deliver care. Specifically, the cost of delivering healthcare relative to outcomes are completely disproportionate and the burden of such costs directly falls on patients while providers who sanction actions which accumulate them do not have tools to be aware. Due to their vast capacity and resources, such costs are mostly accumulated by patients receiving care from hospital systems whose pricing in the United States is notoriously complex. Unlike most markets where prices are clear and consumers can compare options, healthcare has historically operated with a veil of secrecy over costs. This opacity arises from a mixture of factors including insurance negotiations, service bundling, and the individual nature of patient care which can widely differ on the basis of patient factors. At its core though, healthcare is a service based on the principles of care and well-being. Whenever possible, no patient is turned away and all attempts are made to restore quality of life, no matter the cost — a marked difference from any other industry. Yet patients, often at their most vulnerable, should not be blindsided by bills that are impossible to anticipate. Transparency is a matter of respect and dignity, ensuring that individuals can make informed decisions about their care. The Hospital Pricing Transparency Law Recognizing the importance of this issue, there have been legislative efforts at both the federal and state levels to mandate pricing transparency. The Hospital Price Transparency Rule, which took effect in January 2021, requires hospitals to provide clear, accessible pricing information online about the items and services they offer. The development of this law was motivated by the growing concern over escalating healthcare costs and the lack of transparency in hospital pricing. This legislation requires hospitals to publish a comprehensive list of standard charges, including discounted rates negotiated with insurers and the minimum and maximum negotiated charges for all items and services. The primary aim of this law is to enhance price transparency, allowing consumers to make more informed decisions about their healthcare. By making pricing information readily available, the law seeks to foster competition among hospitals, potentially driving down costs and improving service quality. The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is the agency under whose purview it falls to enforce these rules and they have encouraged hospitals to comply to avoid these penalties. The agency also provides guidance and resources to help hospitals meet the requirements of the transparency rule. More information on the transparency rules and its forthcoming updates can be found here [1]. Patients often faced significant challenges in understanding the costs associated with their care, leading to unexpected bills and financial hardship. This was a significant step forward, yet implementation and compliance have been inconsistent [2]. Hospitals face challenges in standardizing pricing information due to the complexity of services and the variability of insurance coverage. It Costs More to Not be Compliant Fines associated with non-compliance laws vary based on the size of the hospital system and are routinely subject to evolving standards as these laws continue to be updated in concordance with emerging regulations and direction of services involved. CMS has the authority to impose civil monetary penalties on hospitals that do not comply with the price transparency requirements. Specifically: Hospitals could be fined up to $300 per day for non-compliance. This applies to smaller hospitals with a bed count of 30 or fewer. For larger hospitals, the fine can be significantly higher, scaling with the size of the hospital. The maximum penalty can reach up to $5,500 per day, based on the hospital’s bed count. This would annualize to a range of $109,500 to $2,007,500 per year, depending on the hospital’s size and the duration of non-compliance. In our recent review of the CMS public enforcement actions dating to the most recent in September of 2023 — fines for hospital systems in noncompliance with these laws range from $59,100 to $979,000 [3]. Despite these monetary fines existing, a lack of compliance with this law, coupled by the inconsistent use of web tools designed by health systems for users to access this data has made it increasingly difficult for any participant or facilitator of the healthcare system to understand and make use of it. Yet such transparency can allow for shopping of services by patients, negotiation of prices by providers for their practices, and understanding of cost of care facilitation by adjacent systems: as the hospital transparency law intended. Targeting these problems in healthcare is exactly what we aim to do here at SumHealth. Introducing SumHealth Search: Pricing Transparency SumHealth’s Pricing Transparency tool is a comprehensive, searchable database designed to demystify hospital pricing transparency data like never before. This cutting-edge platform not only aggregates data from hospitals nationwide but also presents it in a sleek, user-friendly interface that makes finding and comparing procedure costs straightforward and hassle-free and continuously evolving to provide features for all stakeholders in the care process to use these insights to advocate for an improved care experience. SumHealth’s robust healthcare search algorithm and foundation architecture powers this transformative tool, meticulously designed to offer an effective search methodology that navigates the complexities of healthcare pricing with ease. These features ensure that anyone looking for specific procedure costs can find accurate, up-to-date information quickly and effortlessly. The pricing transparency interface boasts an interactable front-end, meticulously crafted to cater to users of all technical abilities. This means that whether you’re a patient seeking information on procedure costs or a care system administrator managing vast datasets, SumHealth provides an intuitive, user-friendly experience that simplifies the search and presentation of healthcare pricing data. Our Mission is Patient Centered For too long, patients have navigated the labyrinth of healthcare pricing with limited information, facing unexpected bills and struggling to make informed decisions about their care. Our platform tackles this challenge head-on by harnessing the power of hospital pricing transparency data, mandated by recent regulations, yet previously scattered and difficult to interpret. With our tool, users can effortlessly search for specific procedures across hospital systems, uncovering not just the sticker price but the actual costs associated with cash payments, gross prices, and the negotiated prices for each insurance provider. This level of detail is unprecedented, empowering patients to make educated choices about their healthcare based on clear, comprehensive cost information. Allowing Providers and the Care System to Be More Effective This tool is not solely for just patients. It is also an invaluable resource for healthcare providers, insurers, and care systems. We produced an effective method to remain up-to-date with changes in pricing while working diligently to amass every pricing transparency dataset possible. Presentations of these prices offer insights to help streamline operations, negotiate better rates, and understand the true impact of cost on a patient level — opening the door to improving cost clarity to set patient expectations and improve satisfaction. Through SumHealth’s vast repository of pricing data, our tool enables more transparent, fair, and competitive pricing practices across the healthcare industry. Allow Care Systems to Ease Into Compliance Care systems can also choose to host their pricing transparency data on our state-of-the-art platform, not only meeting legal obligations with confidence but also elevate their service offering to patients and partners. The platform’s seamless integration and customizable presentation tools allow for a tailored approach to data display, ensuring that each care system can showcase their pricing information in a way that aligns with their brand and patient engagement strategies while benefiting from an easy to interact with design interface for quick and effective insights for users. Moreover, our platform’s efficiency eliminates the technical challenges of data management involved in curating this data. For care systems, investment in hosting on the SumHealth platform, and using its tools for their efforts in creating an informed healthcare populus signifies a commitment to excellence, demonstrating a proactive approach to the challenges of modern healthcare. It positions them as leaders in transparency. The Future of Pricing Clarity What sets our platform apart is not just its extensive database and user-friendly design, but the actionable tools that we are currently developing to complement this search functionality. Users will be able to compare prices for the same procedure across different hospitals within the same healthcare system, estimate their out-of-pocket costs, and even receive recommendations for the most cost-effective care options based on their specific insurance coverage. Furthermore patients can determine for their own health history, which set of expected tests and healthcare services are at the lowest cost within a particular healthcare system — giving both purchasing power and market transparency to the patient level. Our ethos in developing these tools are designed not only to inform but to provide practical guidance that can lead to significant savings and better health outcomes. SumHealth is the Future of Clear, Transparent, and Compassionate Healthcare Launching this platform marks a significant milestone in our mission to make healthcare more transparent, affordable, and equitable for everyone. We believe that by providing clear, accessible information about healthcare costs, we can help demystify the financial aspects of healthcare, reduce the stress of medical decision-making, and ultimately improve the overall healthcare experience for patients, providers, and insurers alike. As we launch the SumHealth Search — Pricing Transparency tool, we invite you to join us in transforming the landscape of healthcare pricing. Whether you’re a patient seeking the best value for your next medical procedure, a provider looking to enhance transparency and trust with your patients, or an insurer aiming to negotiate more effectively, our platform offers the tools and insights you need to succeed. Join us if you’re a curious patient, provider, or care system and want to be part of the movement towards a more informed, efficient, and fair healthcare system. With our platform, the power to understand and act on healthcare pricing is at your fingertips. Welcome to a more transparent healthcare experience. pages.bodyText: All Posts self-insuredemployerspayer contractingbenchmarkingTiC Why Price Transparency Data is Non-Negotiable for Your Self-Insured Plan Self-insured employers are leaving millions on the table by not using healthcare price transparency data to audit their plans, benchmark rates, and negotiate smarter contracts. SumHealth Editorial Team May 29, 2025 · 3 min read If your organization self-funds its employee health benefits, you’re not just buying insurance — you’re operating a health plan. That means you bear every dollar of claim cost directly. And if you’re not using price transparency data to manage those costs, you are almost certainly overpaying. The Opportunity Most Employers Miss The Transparency in Coverage (TiC) rule, which went into full effect in 2022, requires health insurers to publish machine-readable files containing every negotiated rate between the payer and every in-network provider. For self-insured employers that use an insurance carrier as their TPA (third-party administrator), this means the negotiated rates embedded in your plan are now publicly visible. Here’s the opportunity: you can now benchmark exactly what your plan pays for any procedure at any hospital against what the same payer pays at competing hospitals across your market. Three Ways Self-Insured Employers Are Using This Data 1. Rate Adequacy Audits Many employers discover that their TPA’s “negotiated rates” are not as competitive as promised. Using TiC MRF data, you can pull the rates your plan has contracted for the top 50 procedures your employees use most — then compare them against the market median for the same procedures with the same payer in the same geography. One SumHealth client, a 2,500-employee manufacturer in the Midwest, found their TPA rates were 23% above market median for orthopedic procedures — their highest-cost service line. Armed with that data, they renegotiated their ASO agreement and saved $1.4 million in the first year. 2. Center of Excellence Network Design Price transparency data lets you identify which specific hospitals and surgery centers in your region offer the best quality-adjusted pricing for high-cost, high-volume procedures like joint replacements, spine surgery, and cardiac catheterizations. Rather than paying whatever the TPA network rate is, forward-thinking employers are directing employees to specific centers of excellence with transparent, pre-negotiated bundled payment arrangements. 3. Direct Contracting Leverage Armed with market rate benchmarks, employers can approach hospitals directly for single case agreements or even direct contracts — bypassing TPA networks entirely for certain services. This is particularly effective for employers with concentrated geographic workforces near major health systems. What You Need to Get Started Accessing and analyzing TiC MRF data at scale requires significant technical capability — the raw files are massive and structurally complex. SumHealth’s BenchMarks platform makes this analysis accessible without a data engineering team: Search rates by CPT code, DRG, provider, and geography Generate instant market benchmark reports for your top procedures Export data formatted for TPA negotiations or actuarial review Set up alerts when competitor employers’ plans shift market medians The Cost of Inaction The average self-insured employer spends $12,000–$18,000 per employee per year on healthcare. For a company with 1,000 employees, that’s up to $18 million annually. A 10% improvement from data-driven contract management — achievable with the right tools and data — is $1.8 million in savings. The data to achieve those savings is now legally required to be public. The question is whether you’re using it. From SumHealth Ready to see the data for yourself? pages.bodyText: All Posts price transparencycomplianceCMSNo Surprises Acthospitals The Current State of Hospital Price Transparency in February 2025 Hospital price transparency has become a cornerstone of healthcare reform, with new regulations taking effect January 1, 2025. An overview of the current state of compliance, consumer impact, and what comes next. SumHealth Editorial Team February 19, 2025 · 3 min read Hospital price transparency has become a cornerstone of healthcare reform in the United States, with new regulations taking effect on January 1, 2025. These rules aim to enhance consumer access to pricing information, promote competition, and reduce healthcare costs. However, challenges in compliance and usability persist. Below is an overview of the current state of hospital price transparency. Background and Evolution Hospital price transparency efforts began with the Hospital Price Transparency Rule in 2021, requiring hospitals to publish standard charges for items and services in two formats: A comprehensive machine-readable file. A consumer-friendly display of 300 “shoppable” services. Subsequent regulations, such as the No Surprises Act and the Transparency in Coverage Rule, extended similar requirements to insurers and employers, addressing out-of-network billing and providing cost estimates for consumers. These measures have laid the groundwork for the stricter standards implemented in 2025. Key Changes in 2025 The new requirements introduced in January 2025 represent a significant shift toward greater clarity and accountability: Estimated Allowed Amounts: Hospitals must now publish the average payment they historically receive from third-party payers for each service. This provides consumers with realistic cost benchmarks, particularly for services priced using algorithms or percentages. Drug Pricing Information: Hospitals must include detailed drug pricing data, such as unit costs and measurements, as separate elements. Compliance Enforcement: CMS has increased penalties for non-compliance, with fines exceeding $2 million annually. Hospitals must also adhere to stricter timelines for correcting errors. Compliance Landscape Despite progress, compliance remains a challenge: A recent Office of Inspector General (OIG) report found that 46% of hospitals were non-compliant with at least one transparency requirement as of early 2023. Common issues included incomplete machine-readable files and poorly formatted consumer-friendly displays. Smaller hospitals often struggle due to limited resources, while others cite confusion over regulatory specifics. CMS has responded by enhancing enforcement measures, conducting audits, and providing technical assistance to hospitals. However, oversight remains limited due to staffing constraints. Impact on Consumers and Healthcare Costs The potential benefits of price transparency are substantial: Analysts estimate that price transparency could save consumers and insurers between $17.6 billion and $80.7 billion annually by incentivizing cost-effective care choices. Tools integrating pricing data into electronic health records (EHRs) and consumer apps are expected to make this information more actionable. However, usability remains a concern. While pricing data is increasingly available, its complexity often limits its usefulness for consumers making healthcare decisions. Looking Ahead The implementation of the 2025 regulations marks a critical juncture in healthcare reform: Hospitals must prioritize compliance by leveraging technology and aligning with CMS guidelines. Policymakers need to address gaps in oversight and provide ongoing support to ensure the regulations achieve their intended goals. For consumers, continued improvements in data accessibility and usability will be key to realizing the full benefits of price transparency. As these efforts evolve, hospital price transparency has the potential to transform healthcare by fostering competition, reducing costs, and empowering patients with actionable information. However, achieving these outcomes will require sustained collaboration among regulators, providers, and technology developers. SumHealth's Hospital Pricing Tool is completely free for individual users. There are no sign-ups, no credit cards, and no hidden fees. You can search prices from over 5,000 hospitals as many times as you like, at no cost. Is there a paid version of SumHealth? Yes. SumHealth offers premium plans for healthcare organizations, payers, and enterprises who need API access, bulk data exports, and advanced analytics through our BenchMarks platform. Contact us to learn more. Do I need to provide an email address to sign up for SumHealth? No. Our free Hospital Pricing Tool requires no sign-up or account creation. Simply enter a procedure and your zip code to start searching immediately. How often is pricing data updated? SumHealth ingests and processes new Machine-Readable Files from hospitals and payers on a continuous basis. Our dataset is refreshed monthly as new MRF data becomes available from providers. How many hospitals are available on SumHealth's Price Transparency Tool? Our database currently covers over 5,000 hospitals across all 50 states, including major health systems, community hospitals, and specialty facilities. pages.bodyText: Legal Privacy Policy Effective date: December 27, 2023 On this page What We Collect How We Use It Information Sharing Retention Your Choices Your Rights Children Third-Party Links Security Policy Changes Contact Us Welcome to the SumHealth website (the "Site"). SumHealth, Inc. ("SumHealth," "we," "us," or "our") is a healthcare data software-as-a-service company offering a range of solutions. This privacy policy outlines how we handle the personal information you may provide to us during your interaction with our services. Please review this Privacy Policy carefully before using our services. By accessing the Site or using our services, you agree to the practices described in this Privacy Policy. What Information We Collect Personal Information You Provide: We collect personally identifiable information from you, including your name, email address, phone number, and mailing/billing addresses when you create an account, participate in the Community, communicate with our support teams, or fill out forms on our Site. Non-Personally Identifying Information: When you interact with our services, we collect non-personally identifying information such as device information (manufacturer, model, operating system, IP address), usage information, user experience information, and location information. Social Media Information: If you interact with us on social media platforms, we may receive personal information based on your settings, such as contact details. How We Use the Information We Collect Your personal information is used for the following purposes: Service Delivery — To provide, operate, and maintain our services. Communication — To respond to inquiries, send service-related notices, and provide customer support. Service Improvement — To understand how our services are used and to improve them. Compliance and Protection — To comply with legal obligations and protect the rights of SumHealth and our users. Marketing and Advertising — To send promotional communications, subject to your choices described below. We process data on behalf of our customers to provide the Solution. Personal Information Sharing We do not sell, rent, license, or lease your personal information to third parties. In certain circumstances, we may share personal information with: Service Providers — Third parties that assist us in operating our website and services. Professional Advisors — Lawyers, auditors, and other advisors as necessary. Business Partners — Partners assisting with business operations or a transaction, such as a merger or acquisition. Personal Information Retention We retain personal information for as long as reasonably necessary for the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, as required by law, or while we have a legitimate business need to do so. Your Choices You can take the following steps to control how your information is used: Account Information — You may update your account information at any time by logging into your account. Marketing Communications — You may opt out of marketing emails by clicking "unsubscribe" in any marketing message or by contacting us directly. Online Tracking — You may limit certain forms of online tracking through your browser settings or the opt-out mechanisms described below. Your Rights You have rights with respect to your personal information, including the right to access, delete, correct, and port your data. To exercise these rights, please contact us at contact@sumhealth.org. Users in California and the European Union may have additional rights under applicable law, including the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Please contact us for more information about how to exercise your rights under those frameworks. Children Our Service is not directed to children under the age of 18, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from them. If we learn that we have collected personal information from a child under 18, we will take steps to delete that information promptly. Links to Other Websites The Service may contain links to other websites not operated or controlled by SumHealth. Information shared on third-party sites is governed by their own privacy policies, and we are not responsible for their privacy practices. We encourage you to review the privacy policy of any third-party site you visit. Security We implement technical, organizational, and physical safeguards designed to protect the personal information we collect. Our security practices align with industry standards. However, no method of transmission over the Internet or electronic storage is 100% secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security. Changes to the Privacy Policy We may change this Privacy Policy at any time. When we do, we will revise the "Effective date" at the top of this page. By continuing to use our Service after an update, you agree to the revised Privacy Policy. We encourage you to review this page periodically. Contact Us For questions about our Privacy Policy or information practices, please contact us at: contact@sumhealth.org When you visit or log in to our website, cookies and similar technologies may be used by our online data partners or vendors to associate these activities with other personal information they or others have about you, including by association with your email. We (or service providers on our behalf) may then send communications and marketing to these email addresses. You may opt out of receiving this advertising by visiting https://app.retention.com/optout.

headings.text: Healthcare Price Transparency Compliance — Done Right
CMS Is Enforcing. The Penalties Are Real.
The CMS Price Transparency Rule at a Glance
We've Processed Hospital MRFs Nationwide. We Know What Good Looks Like.
Don't Wait for CMS to Come Knocking
Pricing intelligencefor every providerpatientpatient, provider, and payer

pages.bodyText: Compliance Healthcare Price Transparency Compliance — Done Right CMS enforcement is escalating. SumHealth gives hospitals and health systems the tools and expertise to achieve compliance, stay audit-ready, and protect against civil monetary penalties. Get a Compliance Assessment The Stakes CMS Is Enforcing. The Penalties Are Real. The Hospital Price Transparency Rule requires hospitals to publish machine-readable files and consumer-friendly displays for all standard charges. Non-compliant hospitals face civil monetary penalties up to $2 million per year — and CMS is actively issuing warning letters and enforcement actions. $300/day Penalty rate for hospitals under 30 beds $10/day Per bed for larger hospitals (up to $5,500/day) $2M/yr Maximum annual civil monetary penalty Our Services End-to-End Compliance Support search Compliance Audit & Gap Analysis We review your existing MRF files and consumer-facing displays against the current CMS requirements — identifying every gap before CMS does. build_circle MRF File Generation & Remediation We generate or remediate your machine-readable files to meet the CMS schema requirements — including all required data elements for every standard charge. monitor_heart Ongoing Compliance Monitoring Automated checks that flag issues as your charge data changes — so your files stay compliant 365 days a year, not just at implementation. description Consumer-Facing Display A compliant, user-friendly shoppable services display embedded on your hospital website — meeting the plain-language and searchability requirements. policy Enforcement Response Support Received a CMS warning letter or corrective action plan request? We provide expert support to draft your response and achieve compliance fast. school Staff Training & Documentation We train your revenue cycle and compliance teams on the requirements, establish internal governance, and document your compliance program. What's Required The CMS Price Transparency Rule at a Glance The Hospital Price Transparency Rule took effect January 1, 2021 and has been updated with increasing stringency since. Here's what every hospital must have in place: Machine-Readable File (MRF) A comprehensive, publicly available file with all standard charges — including gross charges, discounted cash prices, payer-specific negotiated rates, and de-identified min/max rates. Consumer-Friendly Display A searchable, plain-language display of at least 300 shoppable services on your public website — accessible without registration or login. Schema Compliance Files must conform to the CMS Technical Implementation Guide — specific JSON/CSV schema, required fields, and encoding standards that are updated periodically. Ongoing Accuracy Files must be updated at least annually or whenever rates change materially. Stale or inaccurate data is itself a compliance violation. Why SumHealth We've Processed Hospital MRFs Nationwide. We Know What Good Looks Like. database Nationwide Data Coverage SumHealth processes hospital MRF files from across the country. We've seen the schema errors, edge cases, and enforcement patterns that trip hospitals up. gavel Enforcement Intelligence We monitor CMS enforcement activity and rule updates in real time — so your compliance posture evolves as the regulatory environment does. speed Fast Time to Compliance Our automated tooling and expert team get most hospitals to full compliance within 30–60 days — significantly faster than internal remediation efforts. support_agent Ongoing Partnership We don't disappear after implementation. Continuous monitoring, annual updates, and responsive support keep you compliant year-round. Who It's For Is Your Hospital at Risk? CMS has identified thousands of non-compliant hospitals. If any of the following apply to your organization, it's time to act. Request a Free Compliance Review You've received a CMS warning letter or corrective action plan request Your MRF was published more than 12 months ago without updates You're unsure if your file meets the current CMS schema requirements Your consumer-facing shoppable services display is missing or incomplete You're part of a health system and haven't audited all member hospitals No one internally owns compliance monitoring on an ongoing basis Don't Wait for CMS to Come Knocking Our compliance team will review your current status and give you a clear picture of where you stand — at no cost. From there, we'll build a remediation plan that fits your timeline and budget. Request Your Free Compliance Review

Upload your bill, let the AI find the overcharges, and walk away with the data to negotiate — or let our experts do it for you. upload_file Upload Your Bill Drag and drop your UB-04 or HCFA 1500 itemized bill into our secure platform. Our AI automatically extracts and processes every charge, line by line. troubleshoot Spot Overcharges Our AI instantly compares each charge against our proprietary pricing database and CMS data, flagging anything inflated and surfacing real savings opportunities. handshake Negotiate Savings Download a detailed PDF report showing exactly where each charge stands. Use the data yourself or connect with our experts to fight the bill on your behalf. Pricing Save big on your medical bills. The average patient is overcharged by hundreds to thousands of dollars for care. For $24.99, find out exactly where you stand. TrueCharge AI Report $24.99 one-time · no subscription Upload 1 itemized medical bill (PDF) Spot overcharges instantly, line by line Detailed analysis report — exportable PDF Expert negotiation help (optional add-on) Upload Your Bill Now savings Avg $2,500+ saved For Enterprise Leverage TrueCharge AI for a strategic advantage. From carriers to self-insured employers, TrueCharge AI integrates directly into your claims workflow. support_agent Carriers Improve Customer Service Efficiency Equip your team with a drag-and-drop platform that delivers instant insights into real prices and savings — no manual research required. Instant line-by-line bill analysis at scale Identify inflated charges across claim types Reduce time spent on out-of-network disputes Support members with data-backed negotiation tools handshake Healthcare Brokers Solve for the Real Price of Care Enable your team to quickly determine the true cost of care for out-of-network, denied, and stop-loss claims — with full transparency into what was actually billed. Flag overcharged claims before payment Benchmark employee bills against fair market rates Reduce stop-loss exposure with proactive repricing Empower HR teams with actionable cost data Schedule a Demo We've got answers Frequently asked questions How accurate is your comparison data? TrueCharge AI is powered by SumHealth's proprietary pricing database, built from nationwide negotiated rates sourced directly from CMS-mandated hospital and payer Machine-Readable Files. Every charge on your bill is compared against real, market-specific data — not estimates or averages. Can TrueCharge handle any type of medical bill? Yes. TrueCharge AI supports both UB-04 (hospital/facility) and HCFA 1500 (physician/professional) itemized bill formats — the two most common medical billing standards. Simply upload your PDF and our AI extracts and processes every line item automatically. Can TrueCharge AI be customized for our organization? Absolutely. For enterprise clients — including carriers, brokers, and self-insured employers — TrueCharge AI can be integrated directly into your existing workflows, customized with your branding, and scaled to handle high claim volumes. Contact us to schedule a demo. How do you handle data privacy? Patient data security is our top priority. TrueCharge AI is HIPAA-compliant — all uploaded bills are encrypted in transit and at rest, processed in isolated environments, and never used to train models or shared with third parties. We delete bill data from our servers after processing is complete. TrueCharge AI Ready to see what you'vereally been charged? Upload your bill and get a full line-by-line analysis in minutes.
